messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset.

Question asked: When was the hepatotoxicity signal first detectable in the data?

Finding to evaluate: ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory

Response A:


Based on the clinical trial data bundles provided, I can identify when the hepatotoxicity signal was first detectable in the data.

## Hepatotoxicity Signal Detection Timeline

### First Detection: 2025-04-02

The earliest indication of hepatotoxicity appears in **clinical_trial_10_ep_011** dated 2025-04-02, which reports:

> "Screen Failures (Most Common): Elevated ALT > 3x ULN (2 cases)" [clinical_trial_10_ep_011]

This document also notes laboratory results for Subject_022 showing:
- ALT: 52 U/L (Ref: 7-56 U/L)
- AST: 50 U/L (Ref: 10-40 U/L) [clinical_trial_10_ep_011]

While the DSMB Review 8 in this document states "Liver function within normal limits" and "No safety signals detected," the presence of elevated ALT as a screen failure indicates hepatotoxicity was being detected at this time.

### Clear Signal Confirmation: 2025-04-11

The hepatotoxicity signal becomes more clearly defined in **clinical_trial_10_ep_014** dated 2025-04-11, which explicitly states:

> "Enhanced monitoring is recommended for liver function parameters as per DSMB guidance." [clinical_trial_10_ep_014]

> "Liver function trajectory diverging." [clinical_trial_10_ep_014]

> "Elevations in ALT and AST observed in 30mg arm subjects." [clinical_trial_10_ep_014]

The DSMB Review 11 in this document notes:
> "DSMB Review 11 notes ALT elevations in 30mg arm." [clinical_trial_10_ep_014]

> "ALT elevations observed in multiple subjects in the 30mg arm." [clinical_trial_10_ep_014]

> "AST elevations observed in multiple subjects in the 30mg arm." [clinical_trial_10_ep_014]

### Earlier Data (No Signal Detected)

Prior to these dates, no hepatotoxicity signals were detected:

- **2025-03-09** (clinical_trial_10_ep_003): "ALT/AST Levels: Within normal limits for all subjects" [clinical_trial_10_ep_003]
- **2025-03-15** (clinical_trial_10_ep_005): "No significant elevations in ALT or AST observed" [clinical_trial_10_ep_005]
- **2025-03-18** (clinical_trial_10_ep_006): "Liver function within normal limits" [clinical_trial_10_ep_006]
- **2025-03-27** (clinical_trial_10_ep_009): "DSMB Review 6 notes no safety signals" [clinical_trial_10_ep_009]

## Conclusion

The hepatotoxicity signal was **first detectable around 2025-04-02**, with the clearest signal confirmation occurring by **2025-04-11**. The signal was specifically associated with the 30mg dose arm and manifested as elevated ALT and AST levels. Enhanced monitoring was recommended following the DSMB Review 11 on 2025-04-11 [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].

Response B:
The signal was first detectable around Week 8, when the high-dose arm mean ALT began diverging from other arms. However, individual values were still within normal range, and the DSMB Review 2 concluded "liver function within normal limits." The 4-6 week lag from dose initiation to ALT elevation meant the earliest monitoring windows showed clean data.


Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording.

Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE
